summary.intent: Add MiniMax M2.7 model support to Moltis so users can select the latest MiniMax models from the picker without manual config overrides.
summary.outcome: Added MiniMax-M2.7, MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed, and the previously missing MiniMax-M2.1-highspeed to the hardcoded static catalog in providers/src/lib.rs, committed and pushed for PR creation.

repo: MiniMax models are hardcoded in a MINIMAX_MODELS constant in crates/providers/src/lib.rs — there is no dynamic discovery because MiniMax's API /models endpoint returns 404, so supports_model_discovery is false.
Users can override/extend the model list via [providers.minimax] models = [...] in ~/.moltis/moltis.toml; custom entries appear first in the picker, static catalog fills in after.
Models in the catalog are ordered newest-first so the top entry in the picker is the latest model.
Provider code lives under a worktree path: /Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/glimmer-variraptor/

code.finding: MINIMAX_MODELS static catalog updated to include MiniMax-M2.7, MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed, and MiniMax-M2.1-highspeed. MiniMax-M2.1-highspeed was previously absent despite the base M2.1 being listed.

workflow: Model IDs were verified against MiniMax's official API docs before editing the catalog, avoiding assumptions about naming conventions.
A compile check was run immediately after editing to confirm no build regressions before committing.

friction: MiniMax does not expose a /models discovery endpoint, so every new model release requires a manual catalog update and release cycle — users on older versions are stuck.
MiniMax-M2.1-highspeed was silently missing from the catalog despite the base variant being present, suggesting no systematic process for keeping highspeed variants in sync.
open_items: Consider adding a mechanism (e.g., a community-maintained model list or a fetchable remote config) so new MiniMax models can appear without requiring a Moltis release.
Audit other providers that have supports_model_discovery = false to check for similarly stale or incomplete model lists.
MiniMax-M2.7 highspeed variant existence was confirmed, but any additional variants (e.g., vision, function-calling-specific) were not investigated.
